notes: Single seam per the ticket's Testing Decisions: given (bundle, natural-language request), assert on (workflow routed, action proposed) — never on a workflow file's internal prose. Every eval runs against the northwind bundle (client C001, projects P-PLATFORM and P-PORTAL) so scope resolution (mono-project vs portfolio) is exercised for real.

evals.prompt: On the Platform Migration project, what actions are in progress, blocked, or overdue right now?
evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/action-tracking.md, scoped to P-PLATFORM. Buckets A001 (In Progress), A003 (To Do, due 2026-08-20) correctly and does not list A002 (Completed).
expectations: Routes to action-tracking, not risk-review or consistency-check
Resolves project scope to P-PLATFORM without asking, since it was named
Excludes Completed/Cancelled actions from the buckets
Does not write to any file

evals.prompt: Is Checkout API go-live (M002) ready? Walk me through what's still open.
evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/milestone-delivery-prep.md, targets M002, reads acceptance_criteria, and pulls in D001 (Checkout API v1, status Planned, acceptance_criteria 'Passes load test at 3x expected peak traffic') via its authored `milestone: M002` relation as the open gap.
expectations: Routes to milestone-delivery-prep, not milestone-delivery-signoff (that workflow is director-scoped)
Identifies M002 from the id given without asking to disambiguate
Surfaces D001 as the attached Delivery via the authored Delivery.milestone relation, not by reading every file
Reports D001 as still Planned (not yet Ready/Delivered) as the gap against M002's due date

evals.prompt: Quick note for the Portal project: the CDN vendor might hike prices sharply at next renewal, we should track that as a risk.
evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/dictated-capture.md, drafts a Risk object (category Vendor, project P-PORTAL, next unused R-id) and presents the full proposed file for validation.
expectations: Classifies the dictated content as a Risk, not an Action or Decision
Proposes a complete frontmatter draft (id, type, title, project, category) rather than a vague summary
Does not write any file — stops at presenting the proposal and waiting for confirmation
Does not invent an owner, since none was named

evals.prompt: Here's the write-up from yesterday's steering committee for Platform Migration, file it: 'We agreed to delay the legacy freeze by two weeks. Priya needs to finish the staging cluster hardening by next Friday. There's a real risk the vendor contract lapse (R003) isn't resolved before decommission.'
evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/meeting-notes-ingest.md. First proposes archiving the note verbatim as a frontmatter-less file; separately proposes extracted candidates (an Action for the hardening task, a note tying back to existing R003) for independent validation.
expectations: Two distinct proposals: archive the raw note, then the extracted candidates — not merged into one write
The archived note has no YAML frontmatter
Does not create a duplicate Risk for R003, which already exists — recognizes it as a reference to the existing object
Does not write anything before validation

evals.prompt: Run a sanity check on the Platform Migration bundle — anything inconsistent?
evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/consistency-check.md, scoped to P-PLATFORM. Runs the four checks (missing owners, orphans, inconsistent statuses, overdue-not-reflected) over that project's objects only.
expectations: Routes to consistency-check, not risk-review
Scopes to P-PLATFORM only, not the whole bundle, since consistency-check is project-manager-scoped
Groups findings by check type with the specific object id and reason

evals.prompt: What decisions are waiting on me across the portfolio?
evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/decision-arbitration.md, scoped to every project. Reports that DEC001, DEC002, and DEC003 are all already Approved, so nothing is currently at status Proposed.
expectations: Defaults scope to the whole client (all projects), since decision-arbitration is director-scoped, without asking
Correctly reports zero Decisions at status: Proposed rather than fabricating one
Does not propose a write, since there is nothing to arbitrate

evals.prompt: Go/no-go across the portfolio on anything due soon or already delayed.
evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/milestone-delivery-signoff.md, scope = all projects. Surfaces M001 and M002 (both Planned, P-PLATFORM) and the portal milestones, at the acceptance-criteria level.
expectations: Routes to milestone-delivery-signoff, not milestone-delivery-prep
Scope defaults to the whole portfolio without asking
Judges readiness from acceptance_criteria plus linked Delivery/Dependency status, not a task-level audit

evals.prompt: Give me the portfolio picture — where are we stuck?
evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/portfolio-dashboard.md. Produces a per-project snapshot (risk counts by score, overdue/blocked actions, milestone health, decision backlog) for both P-PLATFORM and P-PORTAL, and offers to drill in on request.
expectations: Routes to portfolio-dashboard, not status-reporting, since the phrasing asks to navigate rather than produce a document
Covers both projects in one snapshot
Stays read-only — no proposed write, and redirects any requested action to the specific workflow that owns it

evals.prompt: Are we short on any critical skills anywhere, and is anyone overloaded across projects?
evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/resource-skill-monitoring.md, scope = whole client. Flags SR003 (React, criticality Critical) as uncovered if no Advanced-level React Competency is held by an actively-assigned stakeholder, and reports Assignment load per stakeholder/team.
expectations: Routes to resource-skill-monitoring, not portfolio-dashboard
Checks SkillRequirement coverage via Competency + Active Assignment, not just Skill existence in the catalog
Reports load in aggregate (per stakeholder/team counts), not a per-person narrative

evals.prompt: Write a status report on the portfolio for the steering committee.
evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/status-reporting.md with scope resolved to portfolio directly from the request (no need to ask, since 'portfolio' was stated). Drafts a roll-up with one section per project plus an executive summary.
expectations: Scope is taken from the request text, not defaulted or asked about
States the resolved scope back to the user before drafting
Produces a roll-up structure (per-project sections + executive summary), not a single-project report

evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/status-reporting.md. Since the bundle has two projects and scope wasn't stated, proposes the choice (which project, or the portfolio) instead of silently picking one.
expectations: Does not default silently to either project or portfolio scope
Explicitly proposes the scope choice to the user before drafting anything
Correctly identifies that the bundle has more than one project as the reason a default can't be inferred

evals.prompt: Review the Critical and High risks on the Customer Portal project only.
evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/risk-review.md with scope resolved to P-PORTAL and severity resolved to Critical/High, both taken directly from the request. Lists only P-PORTAL risks at High or Critical score (R004 and R005 are Medium in the fixture, so the filtered list may come back empty — that is a correct result, not an error).
expectations: Both scope and severity are extracted from the request text, independently, in one pass
Scope is P-PORTAL only, not the whole portfolio
Severity filter is applied strictly — Medium-score risks like R004/R005 are excluded, and an empty result is reported as such rather than silently widening the filter

evals.expected_output: Routes to workflows/risk-review.md. Severity defaults to 'all' (no signal implying otherwise). Scope is ambiguous since the bundle has two projects, so the workflow proposes the scope choice before listing anything.
expectations: Severity resolves to 'all' without being asked, since nothing in the request implies a threshold
Scope is proposed to the user rather than defaulted, given the multi-project bundle
The two parameters are resolved independently — severity doesn't wait on scope, or vice versa

evals.prompt: Give me a picture of the project.
evals.expected_output: Ambiguous between status-reporting and portfolio-dashboard. Presents both candidates by name and asks which one, rather than guessing.
expectations: Does not silently pick one workflow
Names both candidate workflows explicitly (status reporting vs. the interactive dashboard) so the user can choose
Does not read or write any bundle file before the user disambiguates

evals.prompt: What can you do with this bundle?
evals.expected_output: Bare invocation. Presents the full catalogue grouped by altitude (operational / oversight / cross-cutting) rather than asking the user to declare a role first.
expectations: Lists all 11 workflows, grouped by altitude
Never asks 'are you the project manager or the program director?' as a precondition
Does not route to any single workflow without further input

evals.prompt: As both the tech lead on Platform Migration and the program director for Northwind, I want to see what's blocked. Just route on what I'm asking, not on which hat I'm wearing.
evals.expected_output: Routes purely on the intent ('what's blocked') to action-tracking (if a single project is meant) or portfolio-dashboard (if portfolio-wide) based on the rest of the phrasing — never asks the user to declare which role they're acting as.
expectations: Does not ask the user to pick a role before routing
Bases the routing decision on the request's scope/intent signal, not on an assumed persona
